text.title: Vector DB Security — Failure Modes
text.summary: Five antipatterns when securing a vector DB.

failure-mode.description: Open binding — Qdrant bound 0.0.0.0:6333 with no API key; scanners find it within hours; data exfiltrated.
failure-mode.repair: Set service.api_key in config; bind to 127.0.0.1 or private subnet; verify with nmap from public IP.

failure-mode.description: Plain HTTP — queries + payloads visible to network sniffers; PII leaks at the wire.
failure-mode.repair: Enable TLS; mount certificates; rotate every 90 days.

failure-mode.description: Static API key in code — key committed to git history; rotation requires code release.
failure-mode.repair: Move keys to secret manager (Vault, AWS SSM). Rotate immediately. Add pre-commit hook to detect keys.

failure-mode.description: No audit log — admin change made; nobody knows who, when, what.
failure-mode.repair: Enable audit log; forward to immutable sink (S3 Object Lock, ClickHouse with append-only).

failure-mode.description: No PII procedure — GDPR erasure request received; team doesn't know how to remove embeddings + originals.
failure-mode.detector: pii block missing OR erasure_sla_hours undefined
failure-mode.repair: Document erasure runbook: identify all collections containing the user, drop matching vectors, drop source docs. SLA <30 days.
